markdown:
#  Which fields in the RFIs tool can be configured as required, optional, or hidden? 
##  Background 
Certain fields in the RFIs tool can be configured as required, optional, or hidden. For example, if you want to make a field required, or hide a section that is not relevant to you and your team, you can configure these preferences in the Company level Admin tool and then apply them to one or more projects. Not all fields are configurable. Fields not available for configuration are in gray italics with the default setting marked in the table. See [Create New Configurable Fieldsets](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/create-new-configurable-fieldsets).
In the table below:
  * A GREEN checkmark ![Icon Permissions Access Check](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/35af6c3878b6d54f67790e6b6491ebf1/713f0933defa9db9317328e2578e5bde/icon-permissions-access-check.png?fm=webp&h=12) indicates the possible settings for the field.
  * A RED 'x' ![Icon Permissions No Access X](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/cdaab801833cf04cee09180aa0f2fef6/a31acd56ba44c1549cf2605afba18b8d/red-x.png?fm=webp&h=12) indicates the field cannot be configured to this setting.
  * A field name in GRAY italics indicates the field cannot be configured from its default setting.


##  Answer 
The table below details the settings that are supported by the configurable fieldsets function in the project's RFIs tool.

title: Which fields in the RFIs tool can be configured as required, optional, or hidden?
description: This table lists the fields used in RFI creation on the RFIs tool that can be configured as required, optional, or hidden by Company Admins.
